finding: A validator or cleanup lane could remove the live imported task ledger file `.atm/history/tasks/ATM-GOV-0196.json` before the governed checkpoint/commit boundary, causing the task lifecycle to lose its canonical JSON state and risking `ATM_TASK_NOT_FOUND` or broken batch checkpoint recovery.
expectedBehavior: Cleanup, validator, and integration command guards must treat `.atm/history/tasks/**`, `.atm/history/task-events/**`, and task evidence files as protected governance ledger state. They may stage or inspect those paths through governed ATM lifecycle commands, but must not delete, raw-clean, or raw-restore live ledger files unless an explicit governed recovery/disposition command owns the exact task scope.
evidenceOrRepro: Observed during the ATM-GOV-0196 batch lane on 2026-07-20 Asia/Taipei after import/claim/validator work: `.atm/history/tasks/ATM-GOV-0196.json` had to be re-imported/re-added before checkpoint, and the current staged recovery bundle includes the restored task JSON plus task-events.
followUp: Add a generic protected-ledger destructive-command guard and regression coverage proving cleanup-style raw commands cannot target `.atm/history/tasks/<task>.json`; keep task-specific recovery for ATM-GOV-0196 separate from the product-level guard.
